theme: Set valves to balance a water network
title: Valve Balancing on a Looped Water Trunk
innovation_hook: Iteratively linearize around the operating point and use loop/cutset structure to pick the minimal set of valves that decouples the outlets.
strategies: no-op baseline: leave every valve open (x=0 everywhere), reproduces the checker's own baseline B

naive linear single-shot control: model each outlet's own trunk pipe as an isolated linear (Ohm's-law) resistor and solve for its resistance directly from the target, ignoring the quadratic law, the parallel bypass, cross-tie reroutes, and downstream coupling

iterative-linearization + cutset selection: run a source-to-outlet min-cut over the valve-capable pipes to find a minimal covering valve set, then Gauss-Newton re-solve the TRUE nonlinear network each iteration (re-linearizing the conductance Jacobian around the current operating point) and backward-prune any valve whose removal does not hurt the objective
